content.text:
CHEGAAR Mohamed
CHEGAAR Mohamed
Grade:Professeur
Grade:Professeur
Email:mohamed.chegaar@univ-setif.dz
Email:mohamed.chegaar@univ-setif.dz
Département:Physique
Bureau:7
Bureau:7

Mohamed CHEGAAR is a professor at the
department of physics, faculty of sciences, Ferhat Abbas Setif-1
University-Setif. He graduated from Constantine University in 1987 in energetics
physics. He completed his “magister” entitled:
‘Effect of non-uniform illumination
on photovoltaic generators’
from Tlemcen University in 1991.  He fulfilled an MA (Licence) of English; dissertation
entitled: ‘
Emergence of radicalism in Colonial America’
from Setif University
(UFC) in 1997. He completed his PhD degree (Doctorat d’état), entitled “Extracting
solar cells parameters and estimating the atmospheric effects on their
performance”
, from Ferhat Abbas-Setif-1 University in 2002. Many of these
research works have been achieved thanks to great helpful people and multiple short
stays in Perpignan University, France; Institute of Physical Electronics (IPE),
Stuttgart, Germany; Center for Solar Energy and Hydrogen Research
Baden-Württemberg (ZSW), Stuttgart, Germany and Lorraine University, Metz,
France. He has been teaching physics since 1987. His fields of interest are renewable
energies and materials modeling. He has been the head of the
solar energy
(photovoltaics and solar radiation)
team in the
Optoelectronic and
Components Laboratory (LOC)
since 2000 and the head of the laboratory in
the period 2016-2019. He has authored or co-authored numerous scientific papers
and participated in many international conferences (see google scholar “M.
Chegaar”). He supervised different, “magister” and PhD theses. He was requested
to review several research papers to
electronics letters
journal,
Applied Energy
journal,
Agricultural and Forest Meteorology
journal,
International Journal of Photoenergy
,
Renewable Energy
journal and
Chinese optics letters
.
